stories.headline: Graham Platner says his past has been ‘weaponized’ at rally in Maine

stories.headline: Iran says staff blocked from entering US after players given World Cup visas

stories.headline: Trump Greets Farmers in Wisconsin, but Says He Could Be Home Watching TV

stories.summary: The Texas attorney general has mounted an all-out effort to prove Democratic Hispanic groups have been corrupting elections. Now he could be the beneficiary of his own attacks.

stories.headline: Chris Davis on His Biggest Investing Mistakes
stories.summary:
Barry sits down with Chris Davis,  Chairman and Portfolio Manager at Davis Funds. They discuss his approach to managing risk and the key elements changing the economy. Chris and Barry also discuss Chris's mentors including Charlie Munger, and how he settled into the family business. 
 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Why Susquehanna Is Building a Prediction Market Business | Odd Lots
stories.summary: Prediction markets that enable you to bet on pretty much everything are everywhere nowadays. But there's still a big question over whether they can expand to include larger institutional investors like hedge funds. Part of the problem is that a lot of prediction market contracts are illiquid and trading volumes can sometimes be shallow. That's where trading firm Susquehanna International Group comes in. stories.headline: RBNZ’s Breman Says Country Finding Solutions to Protectionism
stories.summary: New Zealand’s central bank governor, Anna Breman, is seeing encouraging signs that will help counter the negative effects of deepening protectionism and a decline in global cooperation.

stories.summary: Austria lost its last top credit score from a major assessor because of persistently high budget deficits, ending a decades-long streak as one of Europe’s best-rated issuers.

stories.summary: Indonesia spent much of this week confronting a question that might have seemed unthinkable a few short years ago: what happens when investors stop believing in Southeast Asia’s largest economy?

stories.headline: Amid Graham Platner Controversy, Where Does the Maine Senate Race Go Next?
stories.summary: New revelations about Graham Platner have left Democrats anxious.

stories.headline: Typhoon Jangmi sweeps northwards leaving 23 injured in Japan
stories.summary: More than 1 million people advised to evacuate homes amid 80mph winds and heavy rainTyphoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) moved northwards over the course of this week. From Okinawa to mainland Japan, prolonged and heavy rainfall led to landslide warnings and the flooding of rivers, with Japan issuing level 4 warnings for some rivers, signalling a risk of overflowing. This level is high enough for municipalities to issue evacuation orders.

stories.headline: Albania's Edi Rama tells Euronews there is 'no alternative' to EU accession
stories.summary: In a wide-ranging interview with Euronews, Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama said there is no alternative for his country to EU accession. He also warned about the risks posed by anti-EU forces and “third actors” operating in the Western Balkans.

stories.headline: US stocks slump as fears over Big Tech shake Wall Street
stories.summary: The Nasdaq saw its biggest daily fall since early 2025.

stories.headline: Norway's crown princess on lung transplant waiting list, palace says

stories.headline: China cracks down on soft porn, violence and materialism in viral micro dramas

stories.summary: Chuck Schumer, the Democratic minority leader, has remained officially neutral on the race, but in conversations with donors, he has been clear about which candidate he supports.
